content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA                                    GEORGE DEUKMEJIAN, Governor

DEPARTMENT OF SAVINGS AND LOAN
3460 WILSHIRE BOULEVARD, 3RD FLOOR, LOS ANGELES, CA  90010-2200  (213) 736-2798
MAILING ADDRESS: P.O. BOX 76832, LOS ANGELES, CA  90076-0832
350 SANSOME STREET, 2ND FLOOR, SAN FRANCISCO, CA  94104-1398  (415) 557-3666


Los Angeles
May 16, 1990


Kathryn E. Donovan, Esq.
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

Dear Ms. Donovan:

     In accordance with Government Code §83114, this letter is to
request a written opinion from the Commission with respect to
the following issue:

     Whether the appointment by the Commissioner of the
     Department of Savings and Loan of a former savings and loan
     commissioner as conservator of a state-licensed savings and
     loan association violates any law or regulation
     administered by the Fair Political Practices Commission.

     By way of background, the Department of Savings and Loan
regulates state-licensed savings and loan associations and is
responsible for enforcing the Savings Association Law,
California Financial Code ("CFC") §5000, et seq. The Savings
and Loan Commissioner is the chief officer of the Department
and has general supervision over all state-licensed
associations. As part of its regulatory powers, the
Commissioner has the authority to appoint a conservator for an
association upon finding that certain statutory conditions
exist. The Commissioner may appoint himself/herself, a deputy
commissioner, or any other person as conservator. During the
term of the conservatorship, the conservator essentially
manages and conserves the assets of the association with all
the rights, powers, and privileges of the officers, directors,
and shareholders.

     The Commissioner retains some control over the conservator in
that unless authorized by the Commissioner, the conservator
shall not retain special counsel or other experts, incur any
expense other than normal operating expenses, or liquidate
assets except in the ordinary course of operations. Also, the
conservator cannot order removal of any director, officer or
employee unless the order is approved in writing by the
Commissioner. Also, the conservatorship ends within six
months of the date of the appointment, unless the Commissioner
extends the six-month period. The conservator is paid from the
assets of the association, unless the conservator is the
Commissioner or a Department employee in which case no
compensation is paid.

Kathryn E. Donovan, Esq.
May 16, 1990
Page 2


It is hoped that the above information is sufficient to provide
the Department with the requested opinion. However, should you
have any questions or need further information, please contact
the undersigned at (213) 736-2801 or ATSS 8-677-2801.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]

ALEC C. STEIN
Chief Counsel

ACS:mh

sections.question: whether the appointment by the Commissioner of the Department of Savings and Loan of a former savings and loan commissioner as conservator of a state-licensed savings and loan association violates any law or regulation administered by the Fair Political Practices Commission.
sections.question_synthetic: Does the appointment of a former savings and loan commissioner as conservator of a state-licensed savings and loan association by the current Commissioner violate any law or regulation administered by the FPPC?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section is present. The document provides background information about the Department's regulatory authority and conservatorship powers. Letter is addressed to FPPC General Counsel Kathryn E. Donovan.

embedding.summary: The California Department of Savings and Loan requests a written opinion from the FPPC regarding whether appointing a former savings and loan commissioner as conservator of a state-licensed savings and loan association constitutes a violation of any law or regulation administered by the FPPC.
